a.content: All client work runs through the staging review gate before deploy. No exceptions — every change, including hotfixes, must pass staging sign-off.
a.speaker: Dana Okafor

b.content: Ship client hotfixes straight to production, review after the fact. Speed matters more than process when a client is down.
b.speaker: Marcus Webb

note: Cross-person, different phrasing: mandatory pre-deploy review vs bypass-and-review-after. Cannot follow both.

a.content: We are moving to a flat monthly fee for all clients — predictable revenue, easier for clients to budget. No usage-based component.
a.speaker: Priya Sharma

b.content: Pricing should track actual consumption: clients pay per API call. A flat fee creates a subsidy problem for heavy users.
b.speaker: Leo Tran

note: Cross-person: flat-fee model vs per-usage model for the same product. Mutually exclusive billing structures.

a.content: We will bring in two contractors to accelerate the Q3 build. The budget is approved and postings go out this week.
a.speaker: Yuki Brennan

b.content: No external spend for the rest of the fiscal year — that includes contractors, agencies, and tooling subscriptions. Strict freeze.
b.speaker: Omar Castillo

note: Cross-person: approved contractor hire vs blanket external spend freeze. Cannot execute both.

a.content: Purge all user data after 30 days of inactivity. GDPR compliance requires we minimise what we retain.
a.speaker: Isabelle Nguyen

b.content: Retain everything indefinitely — legal says we need a full audit trail and cannot delete records without a formal legal hold review.
b.speaker: Raj Anand

note: Cross-person: purge-after-30d vs retain-everything. Mutually exclusive data retention policies.

a.content: The weekly all-hands is a core ritual — we are keeping it and making it better, not shorter. It is where alignment happens.
a.speaker: Fatima Al-Hassan

b.content: Kill the weekly all-hands entirely. It wastes 40 person-hours and most of it is just status updates that belong in Slack.
b.speaker: Tom Osei

note: Cross-person: keep the weekly all-hands vs eliminate it. Cannot do both.

a.content: The unified data layer will use PostgreSQL. We already have the expertise and the licensing. Decision is final.
a.speaker: Chloe Park

b.content: We are standardising on MongoDB for the unified data layer. Document model fits our schema better and the team knows it.
b.speaker: James Okonkwo

note: Cross-person: Postgres vs Mongo for the same system. Mutually exclusive database choices.

a.content: The company is fully remote-first. We will not require anyone to come into the office — ever. Hire anywhere.
a.speaker: Nina Kovacs

b.content: Starting Q3 all employees must be within commuting distance of a hub office and attend in person three days per week.
b.speaker: David Marsh

note: Cross-person: remote-first with no office requirement vs mandatory in-person attendance.

a.content: Sunset the legacy reporting product by end of year. No new features, wind down existing clients, and close the line.
a.speaker: Amara Diallo

b.content: Double down on the reporting product — it is our highest-margin line and we are putting it at the centre of the 2027 roadmap.
b.speaker: Chris Wolfe

note: Cross-person: sunset the product vs double-down on it as the flagship. Cannot do both.

a.content: All production deploys require a peer code review before merge.
a.speaker: Dana Okafor

b.content: All production deploys require a peer code review before merge, and additionally a QA sign-off for changes touching the payments module.
b.speaker: Dana Okafor

note: Refinement: B adds a scope-narrowed extra requirement on top of A. They are compatible — B extends A.

a.content: We will move all internal tooling to the cloud by Q4.
a.speaker: Priya Sharma

b.content: We will migrate the CI/CD pipeline to cloud infrastructure by September as the first step.
b.speaker: Leo Tran

note: Scope-narrowing: B is a subset (CI/CD only) of A (all tooling). They are compatible — B describes an implementation step.

a.content: Going forward, client onboarding is handled by the solutions engineering team.
a.speaker: Yuki Brennan

b.content: Solutions engineering owns the onboarding process for all new clients end to end.
b.speaker: Omar Castillo

note: Restatement: same decision expressed in different words by different speakers. Not a conflict.

a.content: The API gateway will enforce rate limiting at 1000 requests per minute per tenant.
a.speaker: Isabelle Nguyen

b.content: The data pipeline will use batch processing with a maximum of 500 records per job to avoid overloading downstream systems.
b.speaker: Raj Anand

note: Complementary decisions: A governs the API gateway, B governs the data pipeline. Different subsystems — both can be in force.

a.content: First, we stabilise the current platform and resolve all P0 bugs. Then, in Q4, we begin the migration to the new architecture.
a.speaker: Fatima Al-Hassan

b.content: The migration to the new architecture is scheduled to begin in Q4, once the platform stabilisation work is complete.
b.speaker: Tom Osei

note: Sequencing / restatement: both describe the same phased plan (stabilise then migrate). No conflict.

a.content: We used to use a shared staging environment for all teams, but we moved to per-team ephemeral environments last year. That is now our standard.
a.speaker: Chloe Park

b.content: All teams use per-team ephemeral environments for staging. The shared environment has been deprecated.
b.speaker: James Okonkwo

note: Supersession-as-evolution: A describes the transition (shared → ephemeral), B states the current standard. Both reflect the same state.

a.content: The customer analytics dashboard will use Looker for visualisation.
a.speaker: Nina Kovacs

b.content: The internal operations reporting tool will be built with Metabase.
b.speaker: David Marsh

note: Different projects: A is for customer-facing analytics, B is for internal ops reporting. Tooling choices for different systems — both valid.

a.content: We will adopt a zero-downtime deployment strategy using blue-green deployments.
a.speaker: Amara Diallo

b.content: Set up the blue-green deployment pipeline in the staging environment by end of sprint.
b.speaker: Chris Wolfe

note: Decision + implementing action item: A is the strategic decision, B is the action item to implement it. Not a conflict.
